claim: Verification of explorer and cross-checker evidence: YOLO evidence system distinguishes four scopes in schema and tests, but acceptance criteria cannot be met because enforcement relies on unverified agent compliance assumptions.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() validates path format but does not validate evidence content was read from that path.
evidence.why: Validation gap: format check without provenance verification allows agents to cite any source regardless of actual origin.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks explicit external scope but does not detect external research disguised as project scope.
evidence.why: Critical enforcement gap: validator only checks explicit scope field, allowing scope mislabeling to bypass validation.

evidence.summary: Test validates external scope blocker but does not test external research mislabeled as project scope.
evidence.why: Test coverage gap: acceptance criteria require comprehensive validation but tests don't cover primary bypass vector.

evidence.summary: Test validates URL-in-path detection but does not test external research without explicit URL field.
evidence.why: Test coverage gap: obvious mislabeling is caught but sophisticated bypass via path fabrication is untested.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs scope separation but provides no runtime verification of agent compliance.
evidence.why: Enforcement depends entirely on agent遵守; no mechanism can detect violations.

assumptions: Agents comply with prompt instructions to keep external research separate from project facts
Agents do not fabricate project paths to cite external web research as project evidence
Agents actually read files they cite with project-scoped paths rather than guessing from web research
Evidence with scope=project and valid path was actually read from that file, not synthesized from external sources
risks: Agents can cite external web research as scope=project by providing any valid project path without URL field, bypassing all validation
Agents can claim to have read project files they actually inferred from web search with no runtime detection
Readiness contract cannot distinguish genuine project file reads from web research cited with fabricated project paths
Acceptance criteria require scope discrimination 'with concrete validation logic' but validation relies on unverified agent behavior

result.notes: BLOCKED: Acceptance criteria require evidence system to 'distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with concrete validation logic' but validation relies on unverified agent compliance assumptions. Cross-checker confirmed enforcement gaps: agents can bypass scope validation by (1) citing web research with scope=project and any valid project path, (2) omitting url field while claiming to have read local files, (3) fabricating evidence that passes format checks without reading cited files. Explorer claim passes on format validation but fails on provenance verification. Tests confirm external scope blocking and URL detection but do not cover web research mislabeled as project scope with fake paths. Critical assumption 'agents comply with scope separation instructions' cannot be verified from project code, tests, or docs and therefore cannot be treated as fact for PRD readiness.
